sections.paragraph:
an unused root, i.q. Ch. סְכָא to look at, to contemplate; Syr. and SAM. ܣܒܐܳ, סכה to expect, to long for; Sam. סכוי an eye. Nearly related to this among the Hebrew roots are, שָׁגַח, שָׁעָה, שָׁאָה No. II, and transp. חָזָה; also, in the Indo-Germanic languages, Gr. θεάω, Lat. scio, Ital. sagio (prœsagio), sagus, sagax, Goth. saighan, Germ. fchauen, fehen).

Derivatives, שֵׂכוּ-שְׂכִיָּה, מַשְׂכִּית.

II.

שכה

also appears to have had the sense of cutting; whence שַׂכִּין a knife, which see.
